insights.title: Morning demand rose faster than the model expected.
One or more hours had a large model error.
The model line rose faster than actual demand.
The actual peak level was higher than the model expected.

tagDefinitions.lag-overheat/cooler-day: lag_24h ramp exceeds recent same-business ramp while cooling load falls, and the served model overpredicts.
tagDefinitions.humidity-ramp: morning demand is underpredicted while humidity or discomfort is high enough to indicate early cooling demand.
tagDefinitions.business-return: today and lag_24h differ in business/non-business type, with a material morning error.
tagDefinitions.intraday-carryover: post-calibration residual carryover worsens the pre-calibration error.
tagDefinitions.freeze: served forecast differs materially from the latest recalculated post-calibration line.

tagDefinitions.weekend-cooling-ramp-underpredicted: non-business midday demand rose under warm or cooling-load context, while the served model underpredicted materially.
tagDefinitions.model-dropped-against-actual-rise: served forecast fell from the previous hour while actual demand rose.
tagDefinitions.rebound-shape-underfit: served forecast hour-to-hour shape was materially below the actual shape.
tagDefinitions.lag-shape-conflict: lag_24h shape failed to support an observed non-business midday rebound.
tagDefinitions.freeze: served forecast differs materially from the latest recalculated post-calibration line.
